title: Node timers/promises imported PromiseLike domain recovery
summary: Open the corpus-prioritized ESM named-import subset of node:timers/promises as PromiseLike receiver proof only. The LibraryApi producer materializes Domain(PromiseLike) for dependency-backed setTimeout/setImmediate imports, but does not emit PromiseSettledValue or recover payloads because timer scheduling and abort rejection remain unmodeled.

corpus.scan: Targeted JS/TS source scan for ESM named imports and CommonJS destructuring requires from node:timers/promises and timers/promises.

opened_capability: LibraryApiContractId::JsImportedPromiseFactory names node:timers/promises and timers/promises ESM imported bindings for setTimeout and setImmediate.
Normalization emits LibraryApi occurrence evidence only when the callee local has ImportedBinding symbol evidence for the exact module/export coordinate.
The admitted LibraryApi result-domain materializer emits Domain(PromiseLike) for setTimeout arities 0-3 and setImmediate arities 0-2.
The existing Promise.then receiver-method producer can then admit .then only through the materialized PromiseLike receiver proof.
closed_boundaries: No PromiseSettledValue is emitted for node timers/promises producers.
setTimeout(value).then(...) does not recover or merge the fulfilled payload without a future scheduling/rejection-aware settled-value contract.
CommonJS destructuring require('timers/promises') remains closed because current JS/TS lowering does not emit static ImportedBinding evidence for that shape.
Namespace/default import member calls remain outside this slice.
Unsupported arities remain closed for result-domain materialization.
Timer delay, ordering, AbortSignal cancellation, and broad scheduling equivalence remain unmodeled.
soundness_notes: Node timers/promises APIs can fulfill with the supplied value, but timer delay/order and AbortSignal rejection are observable. This slice therefore proves only the PromiseLike receiver domain.
The value-graph hard negative covers delay(0, 1).then(x => x + 1): receiver and .then API evidence admit, but the continuation remains opaque without PromiseSettledValue.
